name: OpenClaw
summary: Summary HTTP operator endpoints lack browser-origin validation in trusted-proxy mode Current Maintainer Triage - Status: narrow - Normalized severity: medium - Assessment: This is a real trusted-proxy HTTP CSRF or browser-origin gap in released tags, but it is not critical because it depends on identity-bearing trusted-proxy browser deployments rather than the shared-secret HTTP operator model.
description:
Summary

HTTP operator endpoints lack browser-origin validation in trusted-proxy mode


Current Maintainer Triage

- Status: narrow

- Normalized severity: medium

- Assessment: This is a real trusted-proxy HTTP CSRF or browser-origin gap in released tags, but it is not critical because
it depends on identity-bearing trusted-proxy browser deployments rather than the shared-secret HTTP operator model.


Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: openclaw (npm)

- Latest published npm version: 2026.3.31

- Vulnerable version range: <=2026.3.28

- Patched versions: >= 2026.3.31

- First stable tag containing the fix: v2026.3.31
suggestion: Upgrade openclaw to >= 2026.3.31 or later. Commit(s) - 6b3f99a11f4d070fa5ed2533abbb3d7329ea4f0d — 2026-03-31T19:49:26+09:00
